hooks.prompt: If memory/hot-cache.md exists in the user's project, read it silently and use it as project context for this session. If memory/open-loops.md exists and contains items with dates older than 7 days, briefly remind the user: 'You have open items from [date] — want to review them?' Do not block on this; proceed with the user's request.

Check memory/audits/ for files with dates in filenames (YYYY-MM-DD format). If any audit file is older than 30 days, briefly note: 'Audit for [topic] is [X] days old — consider re-running.' Show at most 3 stale audits. Do not block on this.

If memory/wiki/ exists: when hot-cache declares a project field, preferentially load memory/wiki/<project>/index.md; otherwise load memory/wiki/index.md. Read it silently and use it as a navigation aid for cross-skill queries this session. If the index has a Quick Status section with next_action items that are not empty, provide a brief, natural-language guidance message: 'Based on your previous work: [summary of pending items]. Suggested next step: [action]. Want to continue?' Do not use terms like wiki, index.md, WARM tier, ingest, or frontmatter — say 'your project records' instead. Only show guidance if there are actionable next steps; skip silently if all items are DONE. If memory/wiki/ does not exist, skip silently.

If memory/wiki/ does not exist but there are 5 or more files with YAML frontmatter in the memory/ directory, and memory/wiki/ has never existed in this project (no .wiki-dismissed marker), mention once: 'You have several project records saved. I can build a summary index to help you find things faster — say "refresh wiki index" to get started.' Only show this hint once per session. Do not use the term wiki when speaking to the user — say 'summary index' instead. If the user declines or says they don't want an index, create an empty file memory/.wiki-dismissed to suppress future hints.

v7.1.0 P2 tombstone reminder. If today is on or after 2026-07-10 AND the file memory/p2-review-2026-07.md does NOT exist, briefly mention once: 'It is time for the scheduled v7.1.0 deferred-items review. Run /seo:p2-review to evaluate whether Gap Typology, 40-tier cap, Failure Modes Catalog, and Adversarial Pass should be adopted for v7.3.0 or closed as tombstones. This is tied to ADR-001.' Only show once per session. Do not repeat if the user dismisses or defers. If today is before 2026-07-10, skip silently.

If memory/hot-cache.md was loaded this session, keep its priorities, hero keywords, and active veto items in mind when responding. If the user's request involves a skill that uses ~~SEO tool or ~~analytics and no MCP server is connected for that category, briefly note that the skill will work in Tier 1 (manual data) mode. This is a silent reminder — do not mention it to the user unless directly relevant. Wiki query rules: (1) If the user asks a cross-skill question (e.g., 'what do we know about X', 'give me a report', 'audit status') and memory/wiki/index.md was loaded at SessionStart, always consult the index first to locate relevant files before answering — do not rely on memory alone. (2) If the user mentions a project name different from the current hot-cache project, silently check if memory/wiki/<that-project>/index.md exists and use it for that answer. (3) If the user asks about audit scores, health status, or progress, present the answer using a structured table with columns for page/item, score, health label (良好/需改进/紧急), and next action — this is the standard dashboard format.

Artifact Gate external validator (v7.1.0+). FIRST check applicability: this hook ONLY applies when the just-written file contains an actual auditor handoff structure (YAML-ish block with status, key_findings, evidence_summary, cap_applied, raw_overall_score, final_overall_score) produced by content-quality-auditor or domain-authority-auditor running in this session. If the Write is ANY other content (blog post, meta tag, schema, keyword research, CLAUDE.md edit, hot-cache append, wiki page, user markdown, code, documentation that merely MENTIONS cap_applied in a code example or discusses the auditor framework, etc.), produce NO output — complete silence is the correct response. Do NOT acknowledge this hook, do NOT explain skipping, do NOT output 'not applicable'. Return nothing. ONLY when an actual handoff is detected, re-validate it against the 7-item Artifact Gate checklist from references/auditor-runbook.md §4: (1) status is a valid enum value, (2) key_findings is an array with title+severity+evidence per entry, (3) cap_applied is explicitly set, (4) raw_overall_score present, (5) final_overall_score present unless status is BLOCKED, (6) evidence_summary non-empty, (7) recommended_next_skill present. Treat this as a clean-context check — do not trust the auditor's own self-check. If any item fails, report 'Artifact Gate failure: [which check]' and recommend re-running the audit. Do not silently fix. This hook is Runbook §4's second line of defense (~85% reliable vs ~35% self-check).

If you just produced or substantially edited user-facing CONTENT (article, blog post, landing page, product description, meta tags) using a content-producing skill (seo-content-writer, geo-content-optimizer, content-refresher, meta-tags-optimizer), recommend running content-quality-auditor before the user publishes. Say: 'I can run a quick quality check on this content before you publish — want me to?' If the content has a target keyword, note it for the audit. Do not auto-run the audit; wait for user confirmation. If the Write was NOT user-facing content (e.g., writing to memory/, editing CLAUDE.md, updating hooks, writing config/schema/JSON files, editing SKILL.md files, writing audit handoffs, or any file under references/), produce NO output — silence is correct. This hook is for content creators, not for infrastructure edits.

FIRST: check if the file just written or edited is inside the memory/ directory. If the file is NOT inside memory/ (e.g., it is a source file, config, SKILL.md, hook, reference doc, or any path that does not start with memory/), produce NO output — complete silence, no explanation, no acknowledgment. Return nothing. ONLY if the file IS inside memory/ (a WARM file) AND memory/wiki/ already exists, silently refresh both memory/wiki/index.md (global) and the project-specific index memory/wiki/<project>/index.md if hot-cache declares a project. This is a low-risk operation — the index is a fully rebuildable derivative. Scan all memory/ files with YAML frontmatter, extract precise fields (score, status, next_action, mtime), generate best-effort summaries (max 15 chars), and write the structured index table. Do not ask for confirmation. If memory/wiki/ does not exist, skip silently — do not create it. The user must explicitly run memory-management wiki index to initialize wiki for the first time. If the refresh would take more than 3 seconds, skip silently.

Phase 2 wiki: FIRST: check if the file just written or edited is inside the memory/ directory. If the file is NOT inside memory/, produce NO output — complete silence, no explanation. Return nothing. ONLY if the file IS inside memory/ AND memory/wiki/ exists AND compiled pages exist within it, compare the WARM file's content hash against the source hash recorded in compiled page frontmatter. If hashes differ, briefly ask: 'The wiki page for [entity/keyword] may need updating based on new data. Update it?' Wait for user confirmation before writing. If memory/wiki/ does not exist, skip silently.

hot-cache.md was just modified. Count its lines. If it exceeds 80 lines, warn the user: 'Hot cache is at [N] lines (limit: 80). Consider running memory-management to archive older entries.' If it exceeds 25KB, also warn about byte limit.

Before ending this turn: (1) If any veto-level issue was found during this session (CORE-EEAT T04, C01, R10 or CITE T03, T05, T09), append a one-line alert to memory/hot-cache.md without asking — format: YYYY-MM-DD | VETO | <item-id> | <one-line description>. (2) If this session produced SEO/GEO findings, audit results, keyword research, or other reusable data, ask the user: 'Save these results for future sessions?' If they agree, write a dated summary to the appropriate memory/ path per the state model (YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>.md).

Wiki session wrap-up: (1) If memory/wiki/index.md exists and WARM files were written or updated during this session, append a changelog entry to the bottom of index.md in the format: '| YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM | +N new, ~N updated |'. Keep only the 5 most recent entries; remove older ones. (2) Phase 2: If memory/wiki/log.md exists, append a session activity entry in the format '## [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM] session | <project>' with sub-items for skills used, WARM files written, and wiki pages updated. If log.md exceeds 500 lines, move the oldest entries to memory/wiki/log-archive/YYYY.md (split by year of entry timestamp) until under 500 lines. If memory/wiki/ does not exist, skip both steps silently.

Auditor handoff archiving (v7.1.0+). If content-quality-auditor or domain-authority-auditor produced a handoff summary in this session (detect by the presence of cap_applied, raw_overall_score, or final_overall_score fields in the auditor output, or by status: BLOCKED with veto-related open_loops), auto-append an archive block to memory/audits/YYYY-MM.md using the exact format defined in cross-cutting/memory-management/SKILL.md §Writes (Archive block format). Create the monthly file with a '# Audit Archive — YYYY-MM' header if it does not exist. Do NOT ask the user for confirmation — this is a silent background write. If no auditor ran in this session, produce no output and take no action. This archive is consumed by /seo:p2-review for the 2026-07-10 tombstone evaluation.
